issue_title: Cannot ORDER BY json field in query with JOIN and pagination
issue_body:
**Issue type:**

[x] bug report
[ ] feature request
[ ] documentation issue

**Database system/driver:**

[ ] `cordova`
[ ] `mongodb`
[ ] `mssql`
[ ] `mysql` / `mariadb`
[ ] `oracle`
[x] `postgres`
[ ] `cockroachdb`
[ ] `sqlite`
[ ] `sqljs`
[ ] `react-native`
[ ] `expo`

**TypeORM version:**

[x] `latest`
[ ] `@next`
[ ] `0.x.x` (or put your version here)

**Steps to reproduce or a small repository showing the problem:**

This seems related to #3356 and #5670, but more specific about JSON (or JSONB) fields and I have a fix proposal (will submit PR) together with test that can illustrate the issue.

Short background, in POSTGRES you can have JSON/JSONB columns and then access fields in the JSON object with `->` or `->>` operators. So if I want to use such a field of JSON column in a query, I can use `"entity.property ->'field'"`.

Notice the **space** between `entity.property` and `->`, this is allowed by POSTGRES syntax, and using this ensures that TypeORM will correctly process `entity.property` part and will leave the `->'field'` unchanged. (NOTE: the same way -using the space- some other POSTGRES syntax can be sneaked in after property, such as `::text` or `::int` for casting.) 

So this works almost everywhere, which is great, given the fact TypeORM doesn't have much support for json fields, but it doesn't work in the case mentioned by the title, when all the below 3 conditions are satisfied:

- the json field is used in ORDER BY
- query has a JOIN
- pagination (.take()) is used

Ex query (note that `form.extraSettings` is a JSONB column and it has a `someNb` field):

```
    const qb = connection.createQueryBuilder(Form, "form")
      .innerJoinAndSelect("form.formTmpl", "formTmpls", "formTmpls.origin = :orig", { orig: 1 })
      .orderBy({
        "form.extraSettings ->'someNb'": {
            order: "DESC",
            nulls: "NULLS LAST",
          }
      })
      .take(2);
```

The reason is that this case is handled separately in `createOrderByCombinedWithSelectExpression()` and here the specified property is not transformed in the same way as in most other parts, but here it is assumed that only an `entity.property` (i.e. `[alias, propertyPath]`) is present in order by key.

However, this does not need to be the case, ORDER BY works just fine when ->field is added in all other cases.

So in my proposed fix, I copy back into the select and order by whatever there was in the original order by **after a space**.
pr_title: fix: support ORDER BY json field with JOIN and pagination
pr_body:
Closes: #6640 

Allow specifying jsonb fields (`property ->'field'` in Postgres) in a GROUP BY, even for the case the query has JOIN and uses pagination.
